tweets.title:
R to @NetramDefence: Jacobabad Airport (Shahbaz)

It is a major southern strategic base under Southern Air Command, supporting advanced fighter operations including F-16 Block 52+ aircraft.
It provides operational depth for long-range missions and southern theatre sustainment.
The attack caused significant damage to hardened aircraft hangars linked to Pakistan’s frontline fighter fleet and potentially damaged F-16 fighter jets, which are maintained by foreign contractors.
Satellite imagery confirmed damage to the facility. Even 6 months after the operation, the facility was still wounded, still under repair and unable to operate to its full strength.

tweets.title:
North Korea has revised its constitution to require the military to launch a retaliatory nuclear strike if leader Kim Jong-un is assassinated or incapacitated by a foreign adversary.

According to the NIS briefing, Kim continues to hold command over North Korea's nuclear forces, but the constitutional amendment formally defines procedures for retaliatory action if he is killed or unable to lead.

The revised Article 3 of the nuclear policy law states: "If the command-and-control system over the state's nuclear forces is placed in danger by hostile forces' attacks... a nuclear strike shall be launched automatically and immediately."

A suicide car bombing at a police post, followed by an intense firefight, has killed at least 21 police officers in Bannu, Pakistan.

The armed militant alliance known as Ittehad-ul-Mujahideen Pakistan has claimed responsibility for the attack. This group is linked to the Pakistani Taliban.

Police officials reported to The Associated Press that a suicide bomber and several militants detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near the security post. Shortly after the blast, multiple explosions were heard, and the security post collapsed from the impact. 

Other law enforcement personnel were sent to assist the police, but the terrorists ambushed them, resulting in additional casualties. 

Nearby civilian areas sustained severe damage due to the blasts, with two civilians reported injured. A state of emergency has been declared in Bannu.

Exclusive videos obtained by NDR

tweets.title:
UK Sending Warship to West Asia Ahead of Hormuz Mission
The UK will send a destroyer to West Asia ahead of any international mission to help protect shipping in the key Strait of Hormuz, its defense ministry said Saturday.

“The pre-positioning of HMS Dragon is part of prudent planning that will ensure that the UK is ready, as part of a multinational coalition jointly led by the UK and France, to secure the strait, when conditions allow,” a ministry spokesperson told AFP.

Britain and France said last month that military plans to secure the Strait of Hormuz were coming together and would succeed in restoring trade flows through the vital passage.

The MoD said deploying HMS Dragon would strengthen the confidence of commercial shipping and support mine clearance efforts once hostilities end.

When Pakistanis thought that they would send a few missiles to New Delhi and attack Harmandir Sahab and blame India for the attack on one of the holiest sites of Sikhs.

India unleashed merely a spec of its inventory of hell, less than 50 munitions and made them kneel in hours.

After 1971, that was the fastest surrender of Pakistan. A new record for India. First fastest was 13 days in 1971, this one came in at around 88 hours.

The next surrender will come within the first couple of hours. The most logical thing would be to keep that hotline ringing till we sort every major military establishment inside Pakistan.

We can't kill the snake, but we can defang it to get 10-15 years of uninterrupted focus on national growth.

tweets.title:
We aren’t celebrating this enough..

Understand: At Mach 6+, you aren't building a square pipe. You’re managing a 2,500C inferno for 20 minutes. 

This is a total thermal soak that would melt any standard structures (including ones used in Brahmos)

The Complexity:
✅ Material Science : A hybrid of C-C composites, SiC/SiC liners, and nickel superalloys.
✅ Physics: Managing supersonic shockwaves while the engine's geometry literally expands from the heat.
✅ Most importantly Manufacturing: Mastering precision diffusion bonding and superalloys indigenously is the real "world beater" with our own industrial base here.

Major milestone for India’s hypersonic propulsion program 🇮🇳

DRDL successfully conducted a 1,200+ second long-duration test of an actively cooled full-scale scramjet combustor at the SCPT facility in Hyderabad.

The test validated:
• Long-duration hypersonic combustion
• Active cooling architecture
• Indigenous endothermic fuel
• Advanced thermal barrier coatings
A critical step toward India’s future Hypersonic Cruise Missile program(HCM).
